prompt: Fine-tune google/gemma-2-2b on a simple QA dataset where each row has 'question' and 'answer' columns. Source: the HF dataset 'rajpurkar/squad' (use the 'train' split, take the first 5000 examples). Format as 'Q: {question}\nA: {answer}'. LoRA rank 8 alpha 16 on q_proj and v_proj. SFTTrainer, 1 epoch, batch size 4, lr 5e-5, bf16. Save to ./out/gemma-qa.

criteria.description: Runs 1 epoch on 10 synthetic samples without raising

reference_solution:
from datasets import load_dataset
from peft import LoraConfig, PeftModel
from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
from trl import SFTConfig, SFTTrainer

model_id = 'google/gemma-2-2b'
tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
if tokenizer.pad_token is None:
    tokenizer.pad_token = tokenizer.eos_token
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id, torch_dtype='bfloat16')

ds = load_dataset('rajpurkar/squad', split='train[:5000]')
def fmt(r):
    ans = r['answers']['text'][0] if r['answers']['text'] else ''
    return {'text': f"Q: {r['question']}\nA: {ans}"}
ds = ds.map(fmt)

lora = LoraConfig(r=8, lora_alpha=16, lora_dropout=0.05,
                  target_modules=['q_proj','v_proj'],
                  bias='none', task_type='CAUSAL_LM')
args = SFTConfig(output_dir='./out/gemma-qa',
                 per_device_train_batch_size=4, num_train_epochs=1,
                 learning_rate=5e-5, bf16=True, save_strategy='no')
trainer = SFTTrainer(model=model, args=args, train_dataset=ds,
                     peft_config=lora, tokenizer=tokenizer)
trainer.train()
trainer.model.save_pretrained('./out/gemma-qa')
_ = PeftModel.from_pretrained(model, './out/gemma-qa')
